channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: COD League ‘Most Financially Stable We’ve Ever Been’, Says Activision
item.content:encoded:
The Call of Duty League has, in some ways, had a rocky few years. It fell prey to the same issues that plagued almost every other facet of the esports industry for a while, becoming susceptible to the risky winter that threatened profitability, sustainability, interest, and investment. But, by all accounts, things are now firmly on the up.

In a recent interview with the General Manager of Call of Duty Esports, Daniel Tsay, Insider Gaming Esports learned just how bullish Activision is on the Call of Duty League, and for good reason. In Tsay’s words, things have never been better

Is the Call of Duty League Succeeding?

From an uninitiated perspective, it might not look as though the Call of Duty League is thriving. It’s not a top-tier esports title, but that’s not necessarily a problem. What is an issue is the ever-changing sentiment around each new annual release, some of the competitive elements of the game, and the stability of the wider Call of Duty ecosystem.

Despite these concerns, the Call of Duty League is, as Activision has directly suggested to Insider Gaming Esports, doing better than ever.

That’s from the horse’s mouth of Daniel Tsay, the General Manager of Call of Duty Esports. He has been in his role for several years, leading the competitive charge of Call of Duty across both Warzone and the traditional multiplayer titles, which at the time of writing was Black Ops 7.

Talking to Insider Gaming Esports, Tsay confirmed:

We’ve had to shift in how we execute, but we’ve really concentrated our bets into areas that I think have really propelled this forward.

We’re actually the most financially stable we’ve ever been. Viewership is also the highest it has ever been.

Tsay went on to talk about the prospect of taking the Call of Duty League Championship, the most valuable event in the calendar, out of North America and into Europe. If there’s one thing DreamHack Birmingham has proven, it’s that the Call of Duty esports scene is white-hot in the United Kingdom.

Do you believe that the Call of Duty League is doing better than ever? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that OpTic and FaZe have a killer match lined up at CDL Major 2

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: PlayStation Plus April 2026 Monthly Games Officially Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The PlayStation Plus monthly game additions for PS Plus Essential members have been officially confirmed.

Thanks to insider reports, players already had an idea about what to expect from PlayStation Plus Essential this month. First, it was revealed that Lords of the Fallen will be the headliner in April, and then the second game for the month was revealed soon after. Now, the official confirmation has arrived through Sony’s announcement.

PS Plus Essential Members Receive 3 Titles for April 2026 Monthly Games

The official blog post is out, confirming PlayStation Plus monthly games for April, which players can begin downloading from April 7, 2026, and add to their library until May 4.

First up is Lords of the Fallen, a soulslike action-RPG by CI Games, set in a medieval dark fantasy world. It’s the perfect time to try out the game, with the sequel, Lords of the Fallen 2, expected to arrive later this year for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC via Epic Games.

The second title is Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream, a perfect title for anime fans who are looking not only to experience a single-player story mode featuring an original story, but also to enjoy online modes with lobbies of over 20 players across five parties.

Last but not least, by any means, is Tomb Raider 1-3 Remastered, which brings the original three adventures together in one package, with all expansions and secret levels for modern platforms. It also features upgraded graphics, with the option to switch to original visuals and a new Challenge mode to “replay levels with customized modifiers and complete achievements to unlock 10 New Outfits that enhance Lara’s abilities.”

In other news, the prices of PlayStation 5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal devices are about to increase significantly worldwide this month. Additionally, a tech insider has claimed that the PS6 handheld will have a more powerful GPU than the Xbox Series. What are your thoughts on the PlayStation Plus monthly games for April? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Shift Up acquiring Resident Evil creator’s studio and publishing its upcoming games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Gears of War Voice Actor Says The Coalition Nearly Made Gears 6 Instead of E-Day
item.content:encoded:
Liam McIntyre brought JD Fenix to life in Gears of War 4 and 5. The voice actor has praised the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day showcase and the new direction for the franchise, but has revealed Gears 6 was happening, and he hopes to see the series continue.

Gears of War: E-Day is a 2026 prequel taking us all the way back to the events of Emergence Day and the fallout that ensued. It’s a bold move following recent sequels, and one of the series’ key voice actors has chimed in on Gears and where it was possibly headed.

Gears of War 6 Was Considered Before Gears of War: E-Day

JD Fenix voice actor Liam McIntyre recently responded to the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day presentation taking place after the Xbox Games Showcase.

He’s delighted about the announcement and revealed that he’s ‘pumped’ to see it happening.

When asked if he’s upset about the trilogy not being completed (Gears 6), here’s what he had to say on X:

“I hope they go back to it, but I love those guys and just want them to make a game they feel nails the gears experience everyone loves. That’d be awesome. And hey, if that leads back to the trilogy, Happy days.”

When quizzed about a ‘trilogy,’ McIntyre had an interesting response:

“We did Gears 4 and 5, and there was a suggestion that maybe there would be a Gears 6. But the team wanted to try something new as I understand it. And honestly, it looks great. Can’t wait to play.”

So, there was clearly a sense that The Coalition considered making Gears 6, but decided to go ahead with the Gears of War: E-Day prequel. Not many people are complaining, to be honest, and there’s a high likelihood that The Coalition goes back and develops Gears 6 anyway once E-Day is wrapped up!

Now it’s time for your say: Do you think The Coalition should’ve stayed the course and focused on Gears of War 6 first? Or was a fresh perspective and story with E-Day the right call? Let us know your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out rumors that PS6 handheld’s GPU is more powerful than the Xbox Series S, and the former Call of Duty director who wants to ‘Fix’ Fallout for more hot news.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Invincible VS Open Beta Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times—How To Play, Explained
item.content:encoded:
It’s nearly time to suit up and fight for what’s right, as the Invincible VS Open Beta is just around the corner. It’s the perfect time to give the game a go before its official release on April 30, and there’s plenty of content to enjoy.

From ten playable characters, including fan-favourites like Invincible, Atom Eve, and Omni Man, to six playable stages and three game modes, there’s a ton to unpack in the Beta—you won’t want to miss out.

Whether you’re a fighting game enthusiast or an Invincible fan, the Invincible VS Open Beta is one you can’t miss, so here’s when it launches and how you can take part.

Invincible VS Open Beta Release Date & Start Times

The Invincible VS Open Beta begins on April 9, 2026, and runs for two days until April 11, 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Replaced Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Compared to other Betas, that’s not an immense amount of time, so you won’t want to miss a second of the brawling action. Luckily for you, Insider Gaming has a table showcasing the start and end times for the Invincible VS Open Beta, adjusted for a whole host of time zones for your convenience—check it out below:

How To Play The Invincible VS Open Beta

Unfortunately for PC players, the Invincible VS Open Beta is exclusive to console players, namely those on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

Thankfully, it’s incredibly easy to get involved, as there are no sign-ups or pre-registrations required. Instead, all you need to do is head onto your console’s digital store and search for the Invincible VS Open Beta. Simply download it, and you’re good to go.

  • More Insider Gaming: Hades 2 Console Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

And don’t worry if you’ve got friends across the console pond who you want to play with, as the Invincible VS Open Beta supports crossplay between both PlayStation and Xbox users. With that out of the way, you’re free to beat up any and all of your friends, no matter which platform they play on—in the Beta, of course!

Will you be taking part in the Invincible VS Open Beta? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming release hubs, check out the following:

  • Mouse: PI For Hire
  • Pokémon Champions

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Only 600 Escape from Tarkov Players Have Hit Max Prestige
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov has had a ‘Prestige’ system since 2024, allowing players to level up, hit the requirements, and submit to the system, resetting everything and having another run through the game. With each new Prestige level, players unlock exclusive items, retain their stats, and get special customization options for their character and Hideout.

In a recent social media post, the head of the studio at Battlestate Games, Nikita Buyanov, revealed how few people worldwide have reached the highest possible level in Escape from Tarkov.

The Highest Echelons of Escape from Tarkov

In a post on social media, Nikita Buyanov revealed that just over 40,000 players have secured at least one Prestige level in Escape from Tarkov. To get to that point, users need to have hit level 55, unlocked Kappa, maxed out a couple of skills, and completed a specific questline.

That’s a pretty daunting task for the average player, and it might come as a surprise to learn how many people have hit those requirements multiple times since December 2024, when Prestige levels were introduced. It’s important to stress that Prestige levels persist through wipes (read: game resets).

Nikita confirmed that 24,502 players (as of his post) have hit Prestige 1. From there, the growth drops dramatically, as only 8,946 had hit Prestige 2. The numbers decline rapidly, all the way down to the sixth and final Prestige level. While Battlestate Games has a desire to implement ten Prestige levels, six is the maximum at present.

At the highest level, which would require players to have hit all the requirements six times since December 2024, there are just 593 players.

By comparison, Battlestate revealed at the start of March that more than 33,000 players had completed Escape from Tarkov’s base story. This was introduced in November 2025 when the game hit 1.0, which was widely regarded as the most important moment in the title’s ten-year history.

Are you surprised that only 593 players have hit the max level in Escape from Tarkov? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders’ next map has been hinted at

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: I Got Hands-On With a Gaming Mouse You’ll Never See Again
item.content:encoded:
At DreamHack Birmingham, I was fortunate enough to get face time with Razer, learning about some of the most iconic upcoming items that promise to pad out the peripheral collections of gaming’s most avid aficionados. This included a $500 ‘signature edition’ of the Razer Huntsman, complete with a leather carrying box and limited to just 1,337 units.

But the pick of what I saw was something so rare that I will forever be one of the few people in the world to get my hands on it. It was a critical part of the production of the 20th Anniversary Edition of the Razer Boomslang, the ‘gaming mouse that started it all’ back in 1999.

Razer Boomslang, Made Modern and Hyper Rare

The Razer Boomslang 20th Anniversary Edition is a limited-release version of the legendary mouse from the turn of the millennium, brought up to spec and given a mind-boggling price tag of $1,337. It’s all sold out now, which comes as no surprise, given that it was limited to, you guessed it, 1,337 units.

It’s an iconic thing, boasting Razer’s patented Chroma RGB technology, a handsome charging dock, and the recognizable profile of the original Boomslang, which dropped more than 25 years ago.

The 20th Anniversary Edition also comes with a phenomenal ‘exploded frame’, a separate item that immortalizes each layer of the Boomslang, on display for all to see.

However, the version that I got hands-on with at DreamHack Birmingham transcends that rarity.

I was lucky enough to hold in my hands one of just eight ‘golden sample’ Boomslang mice produced ahead of the core manufacturing of the 1,337 final products. It got even rarer when it was explained that almost every other golden sample was kept under lock and key in Singapore, at Razer’s headquarters.

Put simply, I was grasping a piece of history, remade. The Boomslang was the mouse that changed everything back in 1999, and the 20th Anniversary Edition was doing it all over again, with gaming’s most tried and tested collectors taking immense pride in securing their purchase earlier this year.

What’s the rarest gaming item you’ve ever held or owned? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that COD’s pro players have bagged on Battlefield 6

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Call of Duty Director Wants to ‘Fix’ Fallout
item.content:encoded:
In a recent interview, a former Call of Duty multiplayer director suggested he has something to offer Bethesda Game Studios when it comes to Fallout 5. In his words, the gunplay in Fallout is ‘terrible right now’ and needs shaping up, which is something that a former Call of Duty veteran should have no problem doing.

The segment in the interview was based entirely on a hypothetical scenario. The former director in question, Greg Reisdorf, is not about to waltz into Bethesda and secure a job finessing Fallout 5’s combat systems, but you never know, right?

Greg Reisdorf Thinks He Can Make Fallout Better

In an interview with Hellcase, Greg Reisdorf, who drove the direction of Sledgehammer Games’ multiplayer systems for fifteen years, spoke about a topic out of left field. Later in his interview, the discussion turned to Fallout, with Reisdorf revealing he is a huge Fallout fan.

He said nice things about the television show and expressed his joy at having the chance to work with Bethesda Game Studios on a Fallout x Call of Duty collaboration before he departed Sledgehammer in 2025.

But one thing he isn’t keen on is the gunplay present in Fallout games. He was asked by the interviewer if he would consider offering his services to Todd Howard of Bethesda, to which he said:

I will be there tomorrow, Todd! I will be there tomorrow.

My God. It is terrible right now. Whatever you are doing, do not do what you are doing. There’s so many issues with the animations. Their engine is rough, especially in first person.

I think they use the same models between first and third person. You have to dig in completely into what they are doing in first person. Their whole ADS thing, I think it was click to ADS. It was not a smooth dynamic transitional ADS.

He then went on to talk about how multiplayer wouldn’t necessarily work with Fallout’s VATS mechanic, the aim-assist feature that has been a part of the franchise for many years. He seemed to forget that Fallout 76, a multiplayer-based Fallout title, has existed since 2018.

Reisdorf suggested that Bethesda Game Studios can’t focus on ‘weapons and items’ because they’re potentially spending too much time building ‘such a large world scale’. It’s not easy to have incredible weapons and combat elements when you’re building such enormously detailed environments:

If you are going to do a shooter, the weapons should be the main characters.

Do you think Greg Reisdorf, a former Call of Duty veteran, could lend a hand on the next Fallout title? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Dave Chapelle is coming to Black Ops 7

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.
